name: Irish Christmas Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Dried sour cherries, cranberries, or raisins
1/4 cup Candied orange peel, finely chopped
1 1/2 cups (3 sticks) unsalted butter, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the raisins, golden raisins, dried sour cherries (or cranberries), dried apricots, prunes, candied orange peel, and nutmeg in a bowl.
2. Add the Irish whiskey to the fruit mixture and stir well.
3. In a large bowl, beat the butter until light. Gradually add the sugar and continue beating until fluffy.
4. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then add the lemon peel, juice, vanilla extract, salt, and remaining nutmeg.
5. Mix in the flour, almonds, and dried fruit mixture until just combined.
6. Grease a 10-inch-diameter tube pan with butter and line the bottom with parchment or waxed paper.
7. Dust the parchment with flour.
8. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and spread evenly.
9. Preheat the oven to 325°F (165°C).
10. Bake for about 1 hour and 50 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
11. Brush the top of the cake with remaining whiskey.
12. Let cool for 5 minutes before turning out onto a rack.
13. Brush the remaining whiskey over the cooled cake and let it cool completely.
14. Roll marzipan into a disk, sprinkle heavily with powdered sugar on a work surface, and roll out to a 14-inch round.
15. Turn the cake right side up and place it on a platter.
16. Drape the marzipan over the cake, pressing gently around the sides.
17. Cut a cross in the center of the cake and press the points into the openings.
(Assembly can be completed up to 1 day ahead; cover tightly with plastic wrap and store at room temperature.)
